page: Microsoft Azure E48d_v4 is an x86_64 architecture server belonging to the EDv4 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 48 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 24 physical Intel Xeon 8272CL cores operating at 2.6 GHz. The processor includes a 35 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 (including AVX512F and AVX512_VNNI), AES cryptography, and BMI2. Memory capacity is 384.0 GB, yielding a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core. The instance is bundled with 1843 GB of local storage, though it does not include any GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark evaluations position this server consistently in the average performance tier across multiple workloads. The stress-ng multi-core CPU test yielded a score of 44668.06, while the single-core performance reached 1411.38. Memory bandwidth tests showed a read-only small block cached speed of 2488.71 GB/sec and a large block uncached speed of 165.21 GB/sec. In database and application-level benchmarks, the server achieved 40.15 million operations per second in Redis SET operations and scored 16136.08 in PassMark database operations. Large language model inference speeds are moderate for prompt processing, reaching 1339.93 tokens per second on small models, but drop to a weak tier for text generation, producing 43.69 tokens per second on small models and 7.03 tokens per second on medium models.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the E48d_v4 provides a balanced allocation of high-capacity memory and local storage relative to its 48 vCPUs, making it a viable option for memory-intensive workloads without the premium of specialized GPU hardware. The combination of 384.0 GB of RAM and 1843 GB of local storage makes this server well-suited for relational databases, in-memory caching systems like Redis, software compilation, and static web serving.
description: Microsoft Azure E48d_v4 is an x86_64 memory-optimized server from the EDv4 family, powered by an Intel Xeon 8272CL processor with 48 dedicated vCPUs (24 physical cores) running at 2.6 GHz. It features 384.0 GB of RAM, providing a high 8.0 GB per core ratio, and includes 1843 GB of local storage. Operating under the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, the server supports AVX-512 and AES instructions. Performance benchmarks place the server in the average tier, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 44668.06, a PassMark database score of 16136.08, and Redis SET performance of 40.15 million operations per second. This server is highly suited for memory-intensive workloads, database operations, in-memory caching, and software compilation.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E48d_v4 is an x86_64 memory-optimized server featuring 48 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 384.0 GB of RAM, and 1843 GB of local storage, ideal for database operations and in-memory caching.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E48d_v4 is an x86_64 server with 48 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 384.0 GB of RAM, and 1843 GB of local storage for memory-intensive workloads.
tagline: A memory-optimized x86_64 virtual machine featuring high-capacity RAM, dedicated Intel Xeon processors, and substantial local storage for demanding database workloads.
bullet_points: 48 dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 8272CL processor at 2.6 GHz
384.0 GB of RAM providing a high 8.0 GB per core memory-to-CPU ratio
1843 GB of bundled local storage for data-intensive applications
Supports AVX-512, AVX512_VNNI, and AES hardware acceleration
Strong database performance with 40.15 million Redis SET operations per second
Optimized for relational databases, in-memory caching, and software compilation
